ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Memory.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The faculty of memory is the cause and storehouse of remembrance and recollection.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For memory is a fantasy that is left behind of some sensation and thought manifesting itself in action; or the preservation of a sensation and thought.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the soul comprehends objects of sense through the organs of sense, that is to say, it perceives, and thence arises a notion: and similarly it comprehends the objects of thought through the mind, and thence arises a thought.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is then the preservation of the types of these notions and thoughts that is spoken of as memory.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, it is worthy of remark that the apprehension of matters of thought depends on learning, or natural process of thought, and not on sensation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For though objects of sense are retained in the memory by themselves, only such objects of thought are remembered as we have learned, and we have no memory of their essence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Recollection is the name given to the recovery of some memory lost by forgetfulness.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For forgetfulness is just loss of memory.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The faculty of imagination then, having apprehended material objects through the senses, transmits this to the faculty of thought or reason (for they are both the same), and this after it has received and passed judgment on it, passes it on to the faculty of memory.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now the organ of memory is the posterior ventricle of the brain, which the Greeks call the παρεγκεφαλίς, and the vital spirit it contains.
